desc:
The Bruinen rushes a bit to the south and below through the Valley. North and
above, the graceful Last Homely Home can be seen, with all its terraces and
white arches; with all its graceful trees and its surrounding green, lively
vegetation. The path here was paved with smooth stones and all around are beds
of flowers and lower bushes of all kinds. Here and there, smaller paths paved
with white stones lead through the beds.
name: High above the Bruinen

desc:
All around grow and prosper large, seemingly wild bushes that are interwoven
with thick tangles and ancient vines. Smaller, snaking paths have carefully been
cut through the vegetation while, in a few places, the glimpse of overgrown
marble can be spotted through the leaves; if those stones are part of old
buildings or forgotten statues can only be guessed. The murmur of the Bruinen
can be heard just to the south, while somewhat above and north, sections of the
Last Homely Home are visible.
name: The Old Gardens

desc:
The path leads along the steep ledge. Behind the low, white wall and below, the
Bruinen flows through the wide river bed. Occasional archs of bright stone,
partly overgrown, partly decorated with fine carvings, span over the path.
Everywhere along and on the small wall stand massive pots in which all kinds of
well-tended plants grow, quite many of these plants are herbs, probably used as
spices by the kitchen. A makeshift path twists to the south, leading to the bank
of a small offshoot of the Bruinen.
name: The High Path

desc:
This circular place is maybe ten yards in diameter. It is surrounded by three
rings. The outer ring is a row of seemingly wild bushes that grow leaves of all
shapes and shades of green. The second ring consists of roughly a dozen elven
statues made from white marble, which show only barely visible signs of age. All
of them were created in great detail and seem to carry expressions of immortal
dignity. The inner ring consists of flower beds in which colourful blossoms of
all kinds are to be seen, and all are very pretty to behold. A small wall to the
south puts a stop to anyone falling down the steep slope and into the valley
below, while a path winds itself a few yards up in the north.
name: Place surrounded by White Statues

desc:
This is definitely the most beautiful place of Rivendell. The Hallway,
approaching the room also known as the Hall of the Lost Footsteps, was used to
receive visiting ambassadors, or to host royal wedding celebrations. The
Mainroom illustrates the owner's wish to dazzle and mark his power. The 17
arcades decorated with mirrors that majestically alternate with 17 windows over
240 feet, represent a real technical feat. All is splendor, beauty and
sophistication: the chandeliers, the marble, the bronze, the gilding, and the
large paintings recalling victories.
name: Hall of Mirrors

desc:
This house has been decorated warm and cosy. A bright light illuminates the
entire room, growing brighter further inside. Placed in the centre is a round
wooden table, accompanied by three chairs. A lit and thus warm fireplace
occupies one of the corners. The southern wall is covered with cabinets made of
glass, filled with numerous old and new trophies of war. Various helmets,
shields, and tribe artefacts are displayed along the walls and on pedestals.
name: Mbahdukun's House

desc:
The room is sparse and cloaked in darkness. A slited window lets in a single ray
of light that falls upon the hard, grey-stone floor. The walls are covered with
black curtains that stand still, undisturbed by any draft or breeze. In a corner
of the room is a single bed, hard and uncomfortable. A desk stands here with a
half-melted wax candle, illuminating a feather-pen and ink vial, the desk's only
other contents.
name: The Midnight Sanctum

desc:
Five tunnels converge here at this spacious intersection. To the south one
tunnel makes a steep descent, while the east and north offers wearying ascents.
A crude tunnel burrows deeper into the mountain to the west. Between the
southern and western tunnels a crack in the wall leads down towards a
crude-looking, descending staircase. The area is covered by a thick layer of
dust and dirt, imprinted with wide tracks leading off in all possible
directions. The ceiling is high and the cave is truly vast.
name: Five-way Crossing

desc:
The corridor ascends slightly from south to north. The ceiling arches overhead,
smooth and dome-shaped. Protruding edges and rocks stick out from the cavern
walls in a few places. The stone walls are black as night, and the air is cold
and damp. The dirty, dust-covered floor is marked by a number of footprints that
cross back and forth from south to north.
name: Pitch-black Corridor

desc:
An arched corridor extends from east to west. Green and grey moulds grow over
vast areas of the walls, covering large swaths of the wet, black bedrock.
Boulders and bigger rocks have been left in seemingly random places, most likely
remnants of the excavation process. Small, crumbling, dried-out husks from
insects litter the cavern floor along the walls, and spider webs stretch between
rocks and boulders.
name: Pitch-black Corridor

desc:
The dark stone walls and ceiling creates a dome that covers the entire length of
this corridor. Furhter passage is possible to the east and west. The walls are,
where not smooth, at least fairly even-textured. Traces of bat droppings line
the sides of the tunnel, and a decaying bat corpse lies covered with dust and
maggots next to a small boulder. A light, constant breeze of humid air blows
through the area.
name: Pitch-black Corridor

desc:
This dome-shaped, arched corridor is pitch-black in colour, and has been
excavated using crude methods. Sharp edges protrude at various points from the
walls, and some boulders and smaller rocks lie scattered throughout the hallway.
Footprints have been left all over the area, deeply imprinted into the thick
layer of dirt and dust on the ground. A light breeze blows through the corridor,
spreading a strong, rancid odour. The tunnel rises to the south, while to the
west it descends slightly.
name: Pitch-black Corridor

desc:
The tunnel narrows here, creating a short bottleneck. It widens again to the
east and west. On the western side a massive, round stone door lies cracked on
the ground, its hinges still hanging from the circular doorway. A massive hall
opens up beyond the broken entranceway. To the east the tunnel widens to a cave,
tall and spacious. Remainders of well-gnawed bones, rusted goblets and other
trinkets lie scattered on the ground here.
name: Crude Passageway

desc:
These dark mountain corridors have been mined into a tubular shape: the walls
and ceiling form a reasonably smooth arch. Some bigger rocks and boulders are
scattered throughout the corridor. The stone walls are very dark, the colour
shifting to green and deep purple in places where it is covered by thick sheafs
of mould. The air here is quite humid. The corridor continues both north and
south.
name: Pitch-black Corridor

desc:
The black stone walls of this place are cold and moist, and partially covered
with mould. Thick dust, small insects, spider webs and dried-out husks from bugs
cover much of the ground. Deep footprints cross through here, disappearing to
the east, where the ground becomes dust-free and nearly shining. The ground
slopes steeply, and it looks as if it is slowly being split in two by an
expanding crack in the rock.
name: Pitch-black Corridor

desc:
The tunnel widens as it descends, growing from a small crack above into the
large cavern it is here. Long stalactites hang from the ceiling; they are
massive, with sharp points, and resemble black icicles. Some have broken off and
fallen to the floor, where they lie in pieces. Natural pillars partially block
the view from any point in this cavern, but it is evident that this grand cave
stretches far to the north, east and south.
name: Bottom of a Tunnel

desc:
The ceiling varies in height here, rising far above the cave to the south, and
sloping steeply down to the north. Stalactites of various sizes hang overhead,
covering the majority of the ceiling. A hole in the ground can be seen in the
far north corner of this cave, out of which a sparse cloud of fumes rises. To
the south the cave expands, forming a huge space. Black, moist stone pillars
stand close to the walls, and water is dripping down their sides.
name: End of a Tunnel

desc:
This cave is tall and expansive. Stalactites hang from the ceiling and
stalagmites rise from the floor; in places these have met, forming pillars that
look worn-away about halfway between ceiling and floor. The air is heavy and
moist. Large portions of the dewy cave have been covered by frothy mould. The
black of the surrounding stone shifts into the blue-green of the mould where
this has occurred. The vast cave expands to the south and west.
name: Tenebrous Cavern

desc:
This cave has an odd shimmer to it, with colours shifting from the deepest black
of the surrounding stone to the green-blue of the mould growing on the walls. A
thin, cold mist engulfs the area, carrying a strong odour with it. Condensation
clings to the walls, and stalagmites of various sizes rise from the floor. Old,
cracked skulls and crushed bones lie scattered among the conical calcium
deposits. The vast cave expands further into the shadows to the north and east.
name: Tenebrous Cavern

desc:
Long stalactites and tall stalagmites line the edges of this cavern. The air is
very damp, and a layer of condensation covers the black slate ground. Bugs dart
across the floor, running from fissure to fissure. The odour of decaying flesh
completely fills the area. This giant, deep cave expands further to the north
and west.
name: Tenebrous Cavern

desc:
A black, putrid pond located in the centre of this crudely-mined cave seems to
be the source of the harsh fumes that cloud the air. A rickety wooden staircase
leans against the eastern wall, leading up to a dark cave. The tunnel, dug out
from soft, brown soil, is supported by thick wooden beams and black stone
boulders. It continues in a northernly direction. The area is quite cramped, and
the air is lukewarm and damp.
name: Rancid Pond

desc:
Reinforced by logs and stone blocks, this creaking tunnel makes its way from
south to north. The ceiling is uneven, and small pebbles and chunks of dirt have
fallen from above in many places. The ground is soft, almost wet. Small droplets
of water trickle down the slowly-rotting wooden beams that support the walls and
ceiling. Clouds of thick, mist-like fumes hang like putrid-smelling drapes in
the air.
name: Rough Earth Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel slants slightly here as it slopes downwards from north to south.
Small centipedes and other bugs scurry along the wet, dark-brown dirt walls. The
logs keeping the tunnel from collapsing are slowly rotting, and are
partially-covered by a green-white mould. Debris covers most of the ground:
decaying wooden splinters and partially-burned rope have been trampled deeply
into the sodden dirt floor.
name: Humid Earth Tunnel

desc:
The ground is covered by a thick layer of grey-white dust mixed with brown dirt.
Decomposing, half-eaten bodies have been placed throughout the vaulted crypt. A
hole in the floor in the north-east corner of the crypt opens up into a dank
earth tunnel. The walls here have been hung with femoral and humeral bones as
well as crania, suggestive of a ghastly trophy room. The troll-made burrows
continue into similar-sized rooms to the south and west.
name: Faltering Troll Lair

desc:
The area is absolutely crowded with broken bones, skulls, rags, splintered
tools, and rusted blades. Everything lies in differently-sized piles, in
complete disarray. It is suggestive of a mass tomb. Cockroaches and black-scaled
bugs scurry among the skeletal remains. The ceiling is quite high, and vaults
have been shaped between this cave and neighbouring caves to the north and west.
The walls are lined with skulls; gaping holes have been punched through their
crania, and their eye sockets are thick with spider webs. A narrow chute has
been dug through the eastern wall.
name: Faltering Troll Burrow

desc:
A large stone table has been set in the centre of the room. Wooden stools and
chairs were once here as well, but they have been smashed, their splintered
remains spread across the floor. Broken skeletons and armour line the walls.
Part of the vaults separating the crypts are slowly collapsing, and a number of
thickstone blocks lie on the ground beneath them. Several other lairs and
burrows can be explored to the east and south.
name: Faltering Troll Lair

desc:
This lair is filled with crushed rib bones, broken skulls, and rotting wooden
tools. Small, quick bugs dart from crack to crack as they make their way across
the floor. A massive boar corpse is rotting away in one corner, its head
half-eaten and its ribcage completely exposed. The ceiling is rounded, with
small arches separating this place from the lairs to the east and north. To the
west, rocks, stones and broken logs have collapsed into a huge pile, which is
covered with a thick layer of dust. Space is quite limited here.
name: Faltering Troll Lair

desc:
A smooth, rounded chute makes a steep ascent up to the eastern wall, then
quickly turns northwest. The perfectly circular shape of the chute and the
smoothness of the black stone hint of the chute's frequent use as a conveying
passage. Deep tracks run along the walls and ground, following the direction of
the tunnel. The ceiling is lower compared to the neighbouring caves, and a brisk
gust of wind blows through at irregular intervals. To the west the caves expand
further into the darkness.
name: Narrow Chute

desc:
A big, gaping crack has opened in the centre of the ceiling. On the ground along
the walls lie huge fallen rocks and boulders. Skeletons, crafting tools,
barrels, and rusted weapons have nearly disintegrated beneath the rockfall. A
thick layer of dust engulfs the ruined crypt. The air is heavy, saturated with
ashes and grit. The rubble is arduous to navigate through; there is little space
in which to move. Massive beams of wood have collapsed under the weight of the
mountain, with splinters spread far across the room. A tiny hole can be seen to
the south, between two massive, steadfast boulders. To the east some sort of
troll-made chamber has been dug out of the rubble.
name: Shattered Troll-chamber

desc:
A low-cut cave, with rough ceilings and walls, opens up here. A tunnel leads
downwards: it appears to be dark and cramped, and it quickly turns in a
westernly direction. Flakes of stone are coming off the walls, gathering in
small piles of debris on the ground. A fungus-like, putrid smell chokes the
heavy, humid air. Cockroaches are ever-present on the walls and ground, swiftly
darting into small cracks and holes when disturbed.
name: Dark Grotto

desc:
The damp, cold air that settles heavily here is thick with the distinct scents
of mould and decay. Rough walls and an uneven ceiling form the boundaries of
this grotto which is littered with cracked skulls, discarded foodstuffs and
random debris; all of which have all been swept into the corners. There is an
arch in the northern end of the cave, but no visible passageway: it ends in
another wall. To the west a dark, dust-filled cave can be seen.
name: Dark Grotto

desc:
The rough, low ceiling in here gives off the impression that this is a smaller
cavern than it actually is. To the west a big crack has broken the ceiling and
the walls, leaving a sharp ledge approximately fifteen feet up. Pebbles and
smaller rocks are slowly piling up here. To the east a dark grotto can be seen.
name: Riven Cavern

desc:
A greasy layer of thick, strong-smelling grime covers the roughly-cut walls of
this cavern. The cavern is quite cramped, with large boulders and rocks lining
the walls. Black scales lie crushed on these boulders, and also on the ground.
The air is musty, lukewarm and heavy. A wooden trough of some kind has been
split in two, its broken pieces lying in the centre of the grotto. To the east
another rough-smelling cave can be discerned.
name: Dark Grotto

desc:
This narrow, winding crawlspace is the result of a recent earthquake. Massive
boulders mixed with smaller rocks has crashed down to block what was formerly a
wide tunnel. Wooden beams and sharp rocks point out in all directions, making it
difficult for even the smallest of creatures to make any headway. Only this tiny
passage remains, and it is so small that only a hobbit could possibly crawl
through it.
name: Narrow Crawlspace

desc:
A white marble house has been built on a narrow terrace below the Last Homely
Home. Undecipherable runes have been carved and painted on all possible surfaces
- on walls, on the table, even to the floor. What they all mean remains unknown,
even the language is unclear. There is a painting on each wall, each one
illustrating the same character. The man in the pictures is in his thirties,
dressed in a bright red tunic and has a white frill collar in his neck. He has
two white curlies above his ears, otherwise his hair is short and thin. All in
all this place creates quite an absurd impression.
name: Asclepius' Refuge

desc:
This room is a small and serene sleeping alcove. There is a soft bed, a table,
and some cabinets. A peaceful aura of tranquility embeds the whole room,
welcoming one to sleep safely or to have a refreshing moment of meditation. Two
windows offer a magnificent view towards south and west; one can see statues,
cozy houses, and of course the deep valley where the River Bruinen flows.
name: Sleeping Alcove above the Soothing River

desc:
This path is made of fine, smooth gravel that barely makes a sound, even under
the heaviest of footsteps. A natural barrier made of a variety of low, thorny
bushes and flowering plants protects travellers from a fall to the Bruinen
river, which runs along the trail and fills the air with its soft chant. To the
north, a branch in the path turns onto a steep but negotiable slope.
name: The Path over the Bruinen

desc:
This terrace sits about five feet above the Bruinen. It is encircled by a wooden
balustrade which is finely engraved with floral patterns that evoke the summer
varieties of the valley. The sounds of the river are tempered by the wooden
boards that form the terrace, making the area a calm and relaxing place for
contemplation. A rock wall runs along the northern edge of the terrace; a
natural crack in this wall provides shelter for a myriad of small herbs and
white flowers. In the southwestern corner, a gap in the railing allows an
unobstructed view of the valley.
name: The Summer Terrace

desc:
A broad plateau overlooks a path along the Bruinen. A steep slope descends
abruptly to the west, offering nothing but a long fall to the valley below. On
the plateau itself, there are a few cottages and cabins. Many flowering shrubs
surround them, making the area a lovely place to live and to stroll in. Wildlife
flourishes in the calm isolation of the plateau.
name: The Last Plateau

desc:
A square cottage built from wooden logs stands under three tall trees. Its walls
are covered with ivy a small sand path leads between flowers beds to a front
wooden door surrounded with a pair of glazed windows. There is a living room
with a small kitchen and a big fireplace, a bedroom with a bed and a wardrobe, a
storeroom with a lot of shelves and an underground cellar in the cottage. All
the place is tidy and cosy.
name: A Wooden Cottage under the Three Trees

desc:
The trunk of a massive oak tree captivates the eye. Its bark has had several
large wooden planks attached to it in several places, creating a makeshift path
upwards into the tree, wide enough to even accommodate four-legged creatures. It
leads towards an immense branch that has many offshoots. Time has also created a
trail that leads from the north to the east.
name: Foot of a Massive Oak Tree

desc:
The trunks of a great beech tree stands with many of its kin, the biggest one
save for one oak tree directly to the north by which it pales in comparison. A
rope ladder hangs next to this massive beech tree, allowing one access to the
upper branches. The forest floor is soft and even, with small trails leading to
every tree. A tributary of the river Bruinen lies close by, a pier upon it can
be found to the south.
name: Foot of a Massive Beech Tree

desc:
A small wooden pier offers a sturdy surface over the river tributary. Most of
the wood is quite weathered, warped and faded by the elements, although several
spots at the end of the pier hint of them being repaired recently, as the wood
there is almost pristine. An abandoned bucket rests at the end of the pier,
upturned and cracked.
name: A Small Pier

desc:
Situated at the edge of the plateau, you find yourself in a tidy but cluttered
room with shelving lining almost every wall. Most of the shelves contain books
and scrolls, but there are also a variety of keepsakes from the many adventures
of Faeona. On the west wall, there is a large window overlooking the steep
western slope and the expansive valley below. The windowsill is lined with
colourful rocks, various bones from what appear to be very small creatures, and
slender containers filled with fragrant herbs. On the floor, carpets and
cushions of assorted sizes are piled in configurations suitable for sitting and
sleeping. You feel quite relaxed and welcome in this space.
name: The Bower of Faeona Glynmyar

desc:
Several thick branches form a steady platform to walk upon. A great many of
these branches have offshoots, and although they are not as substantial as the
branch they come from, they still appear to be able to support a great deal of
weight upon them. Most of the bark that one would stand on has been smoothed
down by constant use.
name: Massive Oak Tree

desc:
The branch extends far out from the tree, and at one point it is connected to a
rope bridge that leads to the south. The bridge appears to be very sturdy, made
from quality rope and several pieces of beech wood. It should be able to hold
the weight of many. At a considerable distance from the ground, it provides a
great way to travel from tree to tree.
name: Thick Branch with a Rope Bridge

desc:
Several thick oak branches grow tightly together, their sturdiness forming a
steadfast platform to walk upon. Several structures have been erected upon some
of them, offering a place to rest up high if one should wish. The branches
appear to be maintained regularly, shown by trimmed offshoots and smoothed down
bark, as to not trip or hinder.
name: Massive Oak Tree

desc:
Several branches at this level have grown very closely together, almost
intertwining. The wood used for building the houses and the platform are of the
same colours and patterns as the tree's bark, making it appear as if they are
growths of the great oak. The massive tree that houses all of this appears to be
tower over all the others, which might give a great view on top.
name: Massive Oak Tree

desc:
A great oaken tree features several thick yet flat branches upon which a
makeshift platform has been created. The platform is close by the trunk of the
tree, which has been smoothed in several locations. Several small markings have
been carved into the smoothed bark. A few stubs of what were branches have been
fashioned into small seats, for one to rest upon if required.
name: Massive Oak Tree

desc:
A very thick and heavy branch leads out from the massive beech tree. The branch
ends by going upwards, its tips being used as an anchor for a rope bridge that
leads to another tree to the north. The bridge is made out of quality rope from
the finest elven craftsmen with several pieces of beech wood creating a path for
both animal and humanoids to walk upon.
name: A Thick Branch with a Rope Bridge

desc:
Several planks have been roped onto thick branches, forming a makeshift
platform. many of the planks bear a great deal of scratches and bite-marks,
clearly made by otters, when the tree these planks came from stood by a river's
bank. A very thick rope ladder has been attached to the thickest branch leading
downwards.
name: High up in a Massive Beech Tree

desc:
A great hollowed branch has been turned into a shrine for Yavanna. The branch
ends in a knot to the east, where a large tapestry depicts her as a tall female
in a green robe, crowned with the Sun. She stands in front of a large tree, and
from all its branches a golden dew falls upon the barren earth, which appears to
blossom afterwards. Several small tables are spread across the shrine, each with
small tokens to be offered to the goddess. Two crystal lamps light up the hollow
branch, a reminder of her greatest works.
name: At the Shrine for Yavanna

desc:
A couple of thick and flat branches have grown closely together. Thick and
sturdy rope ties them together, forming a makeshift platform. A few structures
have been erected on the branches, creating residences high up in the tree. The
massive beech tree that houses all of this is surrounded by similarly huge
trees, which mostly block the view. A smaller branch leads to the south, over
the water.
name: High up in a Massive Beech Tree

desc:
A grotesquely large branch of a beech tree has many offshoots that each appear
to lead to a separate treehouse. Some of them are much more lavish than others,
with many embellishments adorned into the wood, while others appear to be in a
state of disrepair. A few signs hang against the massive beech tree, describing
a rope bridge to the north leading to another tree.
name: High up in a Massive Beech Tree

desc:
Thick webs made out of sturdy rope have been hung between several branches. The
oak branches have been reinforced upwards as well with matching wood, giving the
appearance of wooden structures growing out of the tree. A few cloth tarps have
been securely draped over some of the branches, creating resting spots for weary
travellers or visiting citizens.
name: High up in a Massive Oak Tree

desc:
At the top of a massive oak tree, one particular spot has been cleared of twigs
and branches with several planks forming a platform, offering a resting spot
with a magnificent view of the valley. Close by is a grove of matching oak and
some beech trees, although none have reached to the size of this tree, which
towers over all the others.
name: Top of a Massive Oak Tree

desc:
Five great oak boughs that grow close together have been bound together by a
giant net of rope. This provides a makeshift hallway leading to various
treehouses constructed out of different kinds of wood, although they are
predominantly oak just like the tree they are built upon. A roof of slender
branches provides at the very least a semblance of cover from the elements.
name: High up in a Massive Oak Tree

desc:
Thick oaken planks have been tied to great branches, creating a steady path to
walk upon. A few ropes have been securely attached overhead, leading to a small
treehouse. A few benches have been installed against the trunk of the tree,
although there is little view to be had, as the branches and the crafted
treehouses that have been built upon it, block most of the view.
name: High up in a Massive Oak Tree

desc:
An enormous branch has entwined itself with another, forming a small boulevard
for one to walk upon. The oak branches are mostly flat, and have been smoothed
in other areas in order to prevent a nasty fall down. The branches have several
limbs, each forming a narrow walkway to a treehouse. Each has its own different
flair, hinting of a broad range of tastes from its inhabitants.
name: High up in a Massive Oak Tree

desc:
A thick beech branch overlooks a deep part of a tributary of the Bruinen. The
branch is moderately steady, sturdy yet slightly swaying. Although the branch is
not very wide, it could support a fair bit of weight. The massive tree that has
spawned this branch can be found just north, where many of its branches have
been usurped into becoming beams for treehouses.
name: Perfect Diving Spot

desc:
This is a tributary of the Bruinen, so deep that even the tallest elf could
plunge in it and not reach the bottom. The water is clear and mostly still, and
the bottom shows only a bare minimum of rocks. Some large fish can be seen
moving far below, undisturbed by the various ongoings on the water's surface.
name: Deep Part of the Tributary

desc:
Oaken branches twist and turn, and with the aid of a few extra pieces of wood, a
rudimentary treehouse has been created. Small spaces between the branches offer
both a view of the valley on top of fresh air and light. A small carpet covers a
small segment between the bed and the desk, footprints going back and forth
deeply embedded within. One small painting of mallorn trees hangs next to the
door, glowing with a golden hue that is faded in some places.
name: Valacel's Room of Respite

desc:
There is a small pond, surrounded by flowering shrubs with white, purple and
blue colours. The wall is made of natural river stone, while the ceiling is wood
with a skylight just above the pond. A window right next to the door brings in
extra light. A hammock hangs in the northeastern corner, weaved from long and
supple black roots that bend easily and stretch slightly for the most comfort.
name: An Exquisite Sanctuary

desc:
Natural growth of thick roots form three of the walls of this home besides the
one to the west. The western wall is made of fresh timbers that were lightly
stained, including a simple wooden door. Strands of ivy hang from the ceiling,
bright green and leafy, that have grown and weaved their way up among the roots
that form the walls. The floor is covered with an older wood, natural, and
slightly curved as it nears the roots. A hammock is tied in one corner from the
roots for resting.
name: Sudo's Homestead among Roots and Vines

desc:
An incredibly large bed takes centre-stage in this room. Fit for a family of
giants it is covered with silk sashes and pillows of all sizes, furs made for
covers are lining the bottom and a huge purple drape comes from the ceiling
ready to hide the bed completely. A large vanity is set against the top of it
where jewellery boxes are spilling over and an incense burner stands along the
side. See-through silk leggings are draped over the chair in front, a huge chest
filled with boots of all sizes lies in the corner, and suggestive artwork covers
the walls.
name: Naysheela's Parlour

desc:
This little home is made of very thin walls; brown, plain and quite boring! The
branch that cradles this comfortable home is probably weak and thin, as one can
feel it swaying periodically. The weather and elements have not been able to
damage or cause it to fall. An ink stamp is above the door on the west side and
a poem or quote was written in a bold ink on the eastern wall, above a window
that has a prime view of Rivendell below.
name: Bezos's Prime Home

desc:
Lightly stained timber forms the walls of this room, with several windows along
the eastern and southern walls. The eastern wall contains a simple writing desk
inside a large bay window overlooking the tranquil Rivendell valley. The
northern wall is lined with bookcases filled with books, scrolls, and loose
parchment in all manner of size, shape, and condition. A small bed sits beneath
the windows on the southern wall. The door to the west has a silver trim and is
made from a heavier wood.
name: Alariel's Loft

desc:
This spacious lounge has been carved out of rock with a number of windows
providing ambient light through the branches and leaves of the trees outside.
Several lamps of various colours are lit to bring life into the otherwise shady
walls, adorned with paintings and carvings of the celebrated bards of both Elves
and Men. A few musical instruments like harps, lutes and flutes lie scattered on
the thick blankets covering the floor.
name: Dimly-Lit Lounge

desc:
Dark walls of polished brown oak give this room a woodland feel. On one side of
the smooth marble door there are small pegs suitable for hanging a cloak, bow,
and quiver. There is little other adornment, save a small wooden bed in one
corner and a squat wardrobe. The lack of comforts indicates that the home is
used only for a brief respite from a life wandering the wild.
name: Eristor's Humble Abode

desc:
Each wall of this study is covered in bookcases full of dusty, aged tomes. The
martial symbols on the spines of most of the books indicate this is the
collection of a student of the soldierly arts. More books fill piles in the
corners, as well as on the modest table and chairs that are the only furniture
save an old sleeping mat.
name: Small Study

desc:
This a small room filled with plain, but well-made furniture. A bed lines one
wall while a table with a couple of chairs sits in one corner. A stone
fireplace, obviously well-used, sits in the opposite corner. The western wall is
dominated by a large painting of Earendil sailing to Valinor. The gleaming
gemstone on is forehead seems to burst with light.
name: Derek's Alcove

desc:
This is a dank, dirt-covered cave, nestled deep among the very roots of the
mountain. Thick air, polluted by black dust and a ghastly smell permeates the
black cavern. Fragments of bone lie scattered across the room, mixed together
with various bits of wooden and metal debris. The entire area appears to be
sealed off with no obvious exits out to any sort of tunnel or escape route.
Small stalactites hang from the ceiling, and tiny drops of water are slowly
rippling down their sides.
name: Ruined Troll-Hole

desc:
The cavern is tall and wide, and the walls, ceiling and ground are dark as
night. A layer of dust covers most of the area. Smashed barrels and chests lie
scattered all over. Small rocks and pebbles tumble down the walls at irregular
intervals, and piles of rubble are slowly growing beneath the most active of
these rockfalls. The cave turns in a south-to-west direction, creating a
centrally-located natural pillar around which the immediate caves have been dug.
There are no apparent exits away from this litter-strewn, forsaken area.
name: Ruined Troll-Hole

desc:
This walls of this room are crafted from solid stone. On the northern edge of
the room a small fireplace sits opposite a fluffy bed. A wooden desk sits
against the eastern wall, flanked by a tall bookshelf filled with books. The
floor is covered with soft, cosy-looking mats. A window in the southern wall
looks out onto the forest below.
name: Missy's Hideout

desc:
The skylight lounge nestled in the top of the highest tree in the wood, and it
is made entirely out of glass that allows the natural light to come in. The
ceiling is very high and the furniture is very comfortable. There is a small
fire pit in the middle of the room and plenty of places to sit and relax. A bar
is also situated in one corner of the room, and a dance floor in another.
name: Parmitu's Skylight Lounge

desc:
The floor of this room is covered in course sand, mixed with dry mud. The
southern end of the room is open to the sky, giving a clear path for the sun to
beat down within. A pool of dark water lays beneath the window, emanating the
foul stench of death. A few deep tracks lead through the sand into the depths.
name: Alligator's Sunbath

desc:
The centre of the room is occupied by a big table full of books, bottles, and
other items that clearly indicate that this is the house of someone playing with
magic, definitely without a care about tidying up. In one corner lies a single
unmade bed. In front of the bed, hung on the wall, there is painting of an elf
in the middle of a thunderstorm.
name: Raudo's Alcove

desc:
This room is the bedchamber of half-elven war hero Columbia. The room itself has
precise quadrate dimensions, its walls are high and made out of mahogany from
the forest of Fangorn. Burgundy-coloured bookshelves fill the western side of
the room, facing two enormous portraits adjacent to the door. The enchanted
glass floor emits a bright, glowing light, creating a warm and cosy atmosphere.
name: Columbia's Condo

desc:
An echoing and humming sound is heard upon entering this sacred place which is
surrounded by a very faint glow. The bedroom of the High Priest Columbia is a
peaceful sanctum for both meditation and wisdom. The space here is full of oak
furniture containing ancient scrolls, crystals, and artifacts that emit a soft
blue glow. The low walls, mahogany floor and mirror ceiling create an alluring
warm atmosphere for study and resting.
name: Sanctuary

desc:
This chamber is made out of wood from some black pine tree. The dark colours of
the room are contrasted by a marble altar standing in the middle of the room.
There is a bed in one of the corners, a desk with a chair next to the window,
and a large bookshelf standing against one of the walls. The window shows the
beautiful scenery of Rivendell.
name: Mirasantika's Chamber

desc:
In a silent corner of the winding building is a surprisingly spacious abode,
perfect for a small bed. The room is decorated sparsely with a handful of maps,
many of which are faded and hardly recognisable due to heavy usage. A bookshelf,
worn at the edges as if itself had once been read from cover to cover, is the
other singular piece of furniture. A tidy and clean atmosphere sets the
surroundings. A selection of aged, yet clean rugs, slightly mismatched in colour
have been placed carefully across the floor, small areas of pine appearing among
the gaps, creating the impression of wooden slats between the fading colours.
Cutting across the ceiling is a single bolt of lightning, carved into the
granite roof, appearing to lack the deliberate care of the rest of the room.
name: Maling's Chamber of Lightning

desc:
The timber-framed building has a thatched roof, with a detailed cornice along
all of the walls that borders the ceiling. The ornamental cornice has several
oak leaves carved upon it. The flooring materials that were used appear to be
made of some type of wood that has a rare, chalky-white finish and slightly
textured.
name: Sanson's Tree House

desc:
The memorial shrine is lit with torches that lead down a short hall to a statue
of the finest dark marble. The statue stands at the end of the hall under a dome
that opens upwards. The marble dome has been masterfully covered in murals
depicting the valiant life of Tahimik Pagtulog from childhood to fatherhood.
name: Memorial Shrine

desc:
This relaxing lounge is dominated by lush vegetation and potted plants. An elf
who loves nature and growing things clearly spends a lot of time here. In the
middle of the room is an ornate table with a large jar made of the finest
silver. Leaning closer, the smell of the best pipe-weed the shire can produce
permeates the air here. A carved wooden rack filled with different pipes are on
display and comfy chairs are placed about the room. Near the rack is a small
opening in the floor leading into the room below, with intertwined vines that
are being used as a ladder.
name: Jaak's Smoking Lounge

desc:
A row of prismatic crystal lanterns beams a brilliant rainbow of light
throughout the brightly coloured room. In one corner, a shrine to the Maia
Dawnborn sits, covered in the dust of disuse. A low couch dominates one side of
the room where esoteric magic tomes are piled. A faint scent of Old Toby fills
the air around the couch. A cabinet for arms and armour occupies another part of
the room, while artfully placed mirrors scatter brilliant light in a seemingly
infinite display of rainbow brilliance.
name: Aurora's Brilliant Prism among the Clouds

desc:
A room full of heavy ironbound chests and piles of assorted valuables, scattered
herbs, and detritus at first suggest the holdings of a somewhat organised troll,
but this is the wealth of a compulsive elf. Almost every part of the room is
filled with enough wealth and supplies to equip armies, yet it gathers dust to
soothe the ego of the owner. In one corner, a bed covered in cloaks and armour
sits, with just enough room cleared to barely sleep. A pile of lembas nearby
with some half empty wine bottles completes the scene.
name: Ayl's Hall of Elven Treasures and Trinkets

desc:
Perched atop a colossal, ancient tree, a realm of magic unfolds for the esteemed
ancient magic mistress. This celestial chamber, reminiscent of Elven
sanctuaries, gazes upon a breathtaking vista-a meandering river flowing through
a lush forest, evoking the very essence of Middle-earth. Ethereal light bathes
the room, filtering through intricate stained glass windows, casting a delicate
glow upon the room. Elven tapestries, adorned with intricate designs, depict the
tales of old. Soft whispers of enchantments linger in the air, above a
throne-like chair, carved with elvish motifs. It is a place for deep meditation
that allows communing with the ancient magic of Valinor and harnessing the power
that echoes through the land of elves.
name: Akasha's Meditation Chamber

desc:
Perched high on a towering, ancient tree in Rivendell, a meditation room exudes
enchantment. Its panoramic view reveals a lush, verdant forest and meandering
rivers that glisten under the sun's gentle caress. Soft rays of celestial light
filter through intricate windows, suffusing the space with a magical glow.
Whispers of ancient spells linger in the air, inviting tranquility and deep
introspection. Here, amidst nature's embrace and mystical ambiance, one can find
solace and embark on a profound journey of meditation and spiritual awakening.
name: Prithvi's Meditation Chamber

desc:
Nestled at the pinnacle of the ancient tree, the dimly-lit chapel emanates an
enchanting, mystical ambiance. Muted light filters through the foliage, casting
haunting shadows upon the weathered timber walls. Flickering candles illuminate
cryptic symbols, evoking a sense of ancient sorcery and drawing visitors into a
realm where dark secrets intertwine with arcane wonders.
name: Dimly-Lit Chapel

desc:
The Arcane Chamber, perched atop an ancient tree in Rivendell, emanates a
powerful and enchanting aura. Overlooking the lush forest and meandering river,
this sacred space brims with mystical energy. Elaborate symbols adorn the walls,
their magical essence shimmering with every hue. Within, shelves display ancient
artifacts and spellbooks, inviting practitioners to explore the depths of arcane
knowledge amidst the tranquil beauty of nature.
name: Sakti's Arcane Chamber

desc:
Around the room there are several bookcases, each stuffed with books and scrolls
about the arcane and mysterious arts. Beside each bookcase, tall candle holders
cast a soft glow about the room. On the left wall is a large fireplace, its
warmth casting a comfortable peace about the room. Before the fireplace is a
couch of deep purple. Between the bookcases are chairs where one can curl up and
read. A window with a deep sill adorned with cushions looks out at the woods
below.
name: Tink's Library of Arcane Arts

desc:
This humble wooden building seems like a peaceful place. The furnishings are
simple, consisting of a wooden bed and a table with one chair. Each wall has a
painting on it. The eastern mural shows the house of Imladris, while the south
shows the golden woods of Lothlorien. The great realm of Doriath dominates the
northern wall, while the western painting shows a great foaming sea with high
mountains with rising in a land beyond. A fireplace burns in the northeast
corner.
name: Rochben's Sanctuary

desc:
Ainariel Numenesse's boudoir in Rivendell is a circular, star-lit sanctuary with
tall arched windows that bathe the room in celestial light. The walls feature
shimmering murals of twilight skies, while a domed ceiling, inlaid with silver
and mother-of-pearl, mirrors the night sky. At the centre, a luxurious canopy
bed draped in silks and velvets rests, surrounded by ethereal decor, including a
marble vanity and star-themed rugs. Soft melodies and the scent of night jasmine
fill the air, making it a serene retreat for the Beauty of the Stars.
name: Veil of the Evening Star

desc:
This lounge is both home and office to a resident of Rivendell. A woven carpet
lies on the wooden panels of the floor, and natural light spills through ornate
wooden screens and elegant curtains, casting a glow across the room. One wall is
taken up by a bookshelf that rises to the ceiling, laden with well-maintained
tomes and bound papers. Before the shelf is a broad wooden desk, tidily arranged
with maps and writings. An elegant but well-worn chair rests beside it. In the
far corner of the home is a smooth marble altar, draped with a bright
war-banner. Nothing sits on its surface save for a simple golden cup.
name: Nolme's Private Lounge

desc:
Lightly stained timber forms the walls of this room, with several windows along
the eastern and southern walls. The eastern wall contains a simple writing desk
inside a large bay window overlooking the tranquil Rivendell valley. The
northern wall is lined with bookcases filled with books, scrolls, and loose
parchment in all manner of size, shape, and condition. A small bed sits beneath
the windows on the southern wall. The door to the south has a silver trim and is
made from a heavier wood.
name: Fedrianne's Loft

desc:
The high vaulted ceilings in this room are adorned with heavy crystal
chandeliers which cast shadows of light upon the dark hardwood furniture. A long
table, made of rich mahogany, rests in the middle of the room. Surrounding it
are a set of matching chairs covered in lush red silk. Maps are strewn about the
table. Upon the maps are various models ranging from trolls to dragons. Large
tapestries hang gallantly from the walls, depicting scenes of battles of long
ago. At the far end of the table, a grand marble throne sits, engraved with the
past deeds of its owner.
name: Tjemara's War Room
